text: Literally, ‘For thou hast said, Thou (God) takest no pleasure in the right, or what advantage will it be to thee if I sin?’—it seems necessary to suppose that there is an ellipsis in the thought of the sentence here. The sense of Hebrew text is doubtful.

At verse 9, there is such a sudden break in the argument of the chapter that it is difficult to believe the text has come down to us without omission. In the following verse, ‘brought triumph in the hour of darkness’ is literally ‘gave songs in the night’.
